title: TC001-Complete onboarding intake end-to-end and reach diagnostic report on dashboard
description: Verifies a first-time user can complete all 4 intake steps including required resume upload, then finishes processing and sees the diagnostic report on the dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174")
        
        # -> Navigate to the auth page (/auth) to find the sign-up / login form.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/auth")
        
        # -> Force-reload the auth page (add a query param to bypass cache) and wait for the SPA to render interactive elements.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/auth?reload=1")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Diagnostic Report')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The dashboard should display the Diagnostic Report after intake processing completes"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The authentication and onboarding UI could not be reached because the single-page app did not render any interactive elements on the dev server.

Observations:
- Navigated to http://localhost:5174 and /auth (including /auth?reload=1); the page is blank with no interactive elements.
- The screenshot and browser state show an empty/white page and report 'empty page'.

title: TC002-Log in with email and password and reach the dashboard
description: Verifies a user can sign in using email/password and is taken to the dashboard on success.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174")
        
        # -> Navigate to /auth (http://localhost:5174/auth) to find the login form or authentication entrypoint.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/auth")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to /dashboard after successful login."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The application frontend did not load, so the login form was not reachable and the test could not run.

Observations:

title: TC009-Open analysed job in application workspace preloaded from match results

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174")
        
        # -> Wait a few seconds for the SPA to render. If the page remains blank, navigate to http://localhost:5174/auth to reach the login page.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/auth")
        
        # -> Reload the application root (http://localhost:5174) to try to get the SPA to mount, then wait for the page to finish rendering and re-check for interactive elements.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174")
        
        # -> Navigate to the production backend auth page and wait for the SPA to render; if it remains blank, report the test as BLOCKED.
        await page.goto("https://jobhub-production-f138.up.railway.app/auth")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/workspace' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to the application workspace after opening the matched job from match results"
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Job details')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The workspace should display the matched job details as the active context after opening the matched job from match results"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

title: TC012-Save analysed job to tracker from match results
description: Verifies that saving an analysed job from the match engine adds it to the application tracker list.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174")
        
        # -> Navigate to the app's auth page at http://localhost:5174/auth to reach the login screen.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/auth")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Application Tracker')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The application tracker should list the newly saved job after saving it from the match engine."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.fill('nitins@182')
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state

title: TC015-Advance through onboarding steps 1–3 with required fields
description: Verifies the user can progress through the first three onboarding steps by providing the required information for each step.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)

        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/")
        
        # -> Click the 'Unlock my diagnosis →' button to open the onboarding/sign-up flow and reveal the form fields.

        await asyncio.sleep(3); await elem.click()

        # --> Test passed — verified by AI agent
